comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX drafts generated from the current PaperInterface statement surface; definition abbrevs use PaperInterface source comments to expand the source-facing meaning.

definition_rounding_convention.tex_statement: \(\operatorname{roundProb}(p)\) is the binary rounding rule for probabilities with the convention \(\operatorname{roundProb}(1/2)=1\).
definition_rounding_convention.comment: Defines the paper's probability-to-label rounding convention.

definition_collaboration_strategy.comment: A deterministic map from an \(n\)-agent prediction profile to a binary label.

definition_interior_prediction_profile.tex_statement: For a prediction profile \(p\), \(\operatorname{Interior}(p)\) means \(0 < p_i < 1\) for every coordinate \(i\).
definition_interior_prediction_profile.comment: All agent predictions lie strictly inside the unit interval.

definition_non_collaborative.tex_statement: \(\operatorname{NonCollaborative}(C)\) means that there exist an agent \(k\) and a tie label \(\alpha\in\{0,1\}\) such that for every interior profile \(p\), if \(p_k\ne 1/2\) then \(C(p)=\operatorname{roundProb}(p_k)\), and if \(p_k=1/2\) then \(C(p)=\alpha\).
definition_non_collaborative.comment: The strategy follows a single agent's rounded prediction, with a fixed label at that agent's tie.

definition_reliable.tex_statement: For \(n\in\mathbb{N}\), \(\mathrm{Reliable}(C)\) means that for every collaboration setting \(S\), the accuracy of \(C\) on \(S\) is at least the least individual agent accuracy on \(S\): \(\operatorname{Acc}(C,S) \ge \min_i \operatorname{Acc}(i,S)\).
definition_reliable.comment: This is the source reliability predicate over collaboration-setting accuracy surfaces.

theorem_main_no_free_lunch.tex_statement: For every \(n\in\mathbb{N}\) with \(\operatorname{Fin}(n)\) nonempty and every collaboration strategy \(C\), if \(C\) is reliable, then \(C\) is non-collaborative.
theorem_main_no_free_lunch.comment: Equivalently, reliability forces the strategy to follow one fixed agent away from that agent's tie point, with one fixed tie label.
